frozen_components: LLaVA, Llama3-LLaVA, Qwen3-VL base model parameters
architecture.summary: Joint Sparse Autoencoder (JSAE) jointly factorizes sequence-pooled vision and language activations from vision-language models into shared, interpretable image/caption-level features using an explicit alignment constraint. The SAE reads the model's own hidden activations at different layers, learns a shared latent code space across modalities, and enables bidirectional steering through additive feature injection and suppression back into the model.
trained_components: alignment constraint for vision-language feature sharing

biblio.title: Steering Vision-Language Models with Joint Sparse Autoencoders

claims.statement: JSAE recovers cross-modal features for recognizable concepts (e.g., food and animals)
Additive steering peaks at mid-to-late (pre-output) layers and weakens at both ends
Suppression scores remain within a comparable range across all probed layers within statistical noise
Layer-localized effects are related across architectures tested (LLaVA-v1.6-Mistral-7B, Llama3-LLaVA-8B, and Qwen3-VL-30B)
Explicitly aligned sparse representations support more controllable intervention-based analysis of multimodal features within an identifiable layer range than unconstrained alternatives
Applying SAEs to vision-language models often yields representations that are difficult to use as controllable cross-modal steering directions
